text:
are animals used for navigation and travel across vast terrain, usually in Titan-infested territory outside the Walls. They are mostly used on expeditions led by the Survey Corps for hauling supplies and provisions. However, they are also used for leading carriages, hauling carts, and general travel of merchants and civilians within the Walls. Like all other animals, their presence is completely ignored by Titans.

It is virtually impossible to escape the grasp of a Titan without a horse outside the Walls, excluding the Titan Forest where vertical maneuvering equipment can be utilized. Horses stand around 160 cm, weighing between 450 and 500 kg. They can also maintain a speed of 35 kilometers an hour, with their top speed ranging from 75 to 80 kilometers an hour.

Breeding

After the fall of Wall Maria, humanity lost a considerable amount of its territory and resources. In an attempt to make ends meet, the government reached out to rural hunting villages like Dauper in hopes of recruiting them as hired horse breeders.

Biologically, horses were specially bred to allow the Survey Corps to outrun Titans during expeditions outside the Walls. They are not frightened by Titans and are also capable of running long distances without exhaustion. For instance, when Thomas was sent by Mike Zacharias to alert the military following a breach in Wall Rose, he had traveled for eight hours by horse before arriving at Ehrmich District. He then took the fastest horse in Ehrmich to travel for four hours to Stohess to alert Commander Erwin Smith of the breach.

Notable Traits

Speed - They have a top speed of , making them ideal additions to the Survey Corps.

Endurance - They can maintain a constant speed of . Due to their staunch legs, they are capable of withstanding the stress of sudden stops, acceleration, and circling. They are also capable of reaching a speed of while pulling a wagon.

Temperament - They are quite docile and do not normally frighten in the presence of Titans. The nearly constant sound of vertical maneuvering equipment being used so close to the horses also seems to have no effect on them.

Trivia

Horses are noted to be extremely expensive, costing about as much as an average citizen's lifetime earnings. As such, the Survey Corps are usually the only ones to have them.
Like human soldiers, they are considered a part of the Survey Corps. If they die, it is suggested that you bring back as much of its mane as possible for a burial.
